stages.title: Stage 1 · Biological and analytical intent
stages.summary: Use these answers to narrow the collection to workflows that are biologically and analytically compatible with your study before the action sheet adds operational detail.

questions.label: High-level use case
questions.description: Choose the broad application domain that best matches your study. If you are unsure, use the neutral option.

options.label: Environmental metagenomics
options.help: Air or passive water metagenomics, pathogen surveillance, or environmental AMR work.

options.label: eDNA metabarcoding
options.help: Amplicon-based biodiversity or taxonomic assignment workflows.

options.label: Food safety
options.help: Targeted pathogen recovery from food-associated complex samples.

options.label: Clinical isolates / plasmids
options.help: Clinical AMR profiling, plasmid clustering, or host-association analysis.

options.label: Veterinary / zoonotic surveillance
options.help: Avian influenza consensus generation, virology, or host-spillover tracking.

options.label: Viability assessment
options.help: Raw-signal inference of microbial viability using POD5 or FAST5.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Do not use category as a hard filter yet.

questions.label: Sample or material type
questions.description: Pick the sample class that best reflects the material you actually sequenced.

options.label: Air / bioaerosol sample
options.help: Liquid impingement or other air microbiome sampling.

options.label: Passive water / wetland sample
options.help: Water samples from wetland or passive environmental collection.

options.label: Water eDNA amplicon sample
options.help: Amplicon-based biodiversity sequencing from water.

options.label: Food safety or enrichment sample
options.help: Complex food-associated sample with pathogen enrichment or adaptive sampling.

options.label: Bacterial isolate
options.help: Single-isolate or isolate-dominant sequencing for assembly, AMR, or plasmid work.

options.label: Clinical metagenome or swab
options.help: Complex clinical sample rather than a purified isolate.

options.label: Avian influenza sample
options.help: AIV-positive or influenza-targeted sequencing sample.

options.label: Cross-host AIV comparison set
options.help: Matched avian and mammalian host samples for spillover/spillback analysis.

options.label: Raw nanopore signal only
options.help: POD5 or FAST5 used directly for signal-level inference.

options.label: Soil metagenomic DNA
options.help: Useful for nearest-starting-point guidance only if no exact workflow exists.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Do not use sample type as a hard filter yet.

questions.label: Target molecule or signal type
questions.description: Choose the molecule or signal that best reflects the data and analytical branch you care about.

options.label: DNA
options.help: Shotgun DNA, isolate DNA, or DNA-focused metagenomics.

options.label: RNA
options.help: RNA sequencing or workflows centered on viral RNA.

options.label: DNA + RNA
options.help: Dual-extraction workflows where both nucleic acids matter.

options.label: Amplicon DNA
options.help: 12S, 16S, or other targeted metabarcoding amplicons.

options.label: Raw electrical signal
options.help: FAST5 or POD5 signal used directly rather than only after basecalling.

options.label: Viral reads or variants
options.help: Basecalled viral reads, consensus work, or variant files.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Do not use molecule type as a hard filter yet.

questions.label: Main analytical objective
questions.description: Choose the primary reason you need the workflow. This is the strongest route to a specific published pipeline.

options.label: Community profiling
options.help: Taxonomic composition, assembly, binning, and environmental characterization.

options.label: Pathogen / AMR surveillance
options.help: Pathogen detection, AMR profiling, or environmental pathogen screening.

options.label: Viral metagenomics
options.help: Untargeted viral community analysis rather than a single influenza workflow.

options.label: Biodiversity metabarcoding
options.help: Amplicon-based biodiversity or vertebrate host-range detection.

options.label: Target enrichment / Listeria recovery
options.help: Adaptive sampling, target extraction, and Listeria-focused recovery.

options.label: Clinical AMR profiling
options.help: AMR detection and assembly in clinical isolate sequencing.

options.label: Plasmid clustering
options.help: Plasmid typing, clustering, and relatedness analysis.

options.label: AMR host association
options.help: Link AMR genes or plasmids to their hosts using methylation-aware data.

options.label: AIV consensus / subtyping / phylogeny
options.help: Targeted avian-influenza consensus generation and segment interpretation.

options.label: Cross-host variant tracking
options.help: Variant comparison between avian and mammalian hosts.

options.label: Viability inference
options.help: Signal-level inference of living versus dead microbial material.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Do not use analytical objective as a hard filter yet.

stages.title: Stage 2 · Preprocessing and entry point
stages.summary: These answers refine the action sheet: which documented entry point to use, whether basecalling or demultiplexing should still happen, and which read-cleaning defaults to prioritize.

questions.label: Current input format
questions.description: Select the file type you already have in hand.

options.label: POD5
options.help: Raw signal container.

options.label: FAST5
options.help: Legacy raw signal container.

options.label: Basecalled BAM
options.help: Aligned or basecalled BAM input.

options.label: FASTQ
options.help: Basecalled reads in single-end FASTQ.

options.label: Paired-end FASTQ
options.help: Typical for Illumina amplicon input.

options.label: VCF
options.help: Variant file already called upstream.

options.label: FASTQ + VCF
options.help: You have basecalled reads and variant files together.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Do not use input format as a strict differentiator.

questions.label: Library or run mode
questions.description: Select the main Oxford Nanopore kit or sequencing setup that best matches your dataset.

options.label: Rapid Barcoding Kit 24 V14 (SQK-RBK114.24)
options.help: Rapid, transposase-based barcoding for up to 24 DNA samples.

options.label: Native Barcoding Kit 24 V14 (SQK-NBD114.24)
options.help: Ligation-based barcoding that preserves fragment length and aligns with Q20+ chemistry.

options.label: Adaptive sampling
options.help: Real-time read rejection / enrichment.

options.label: Direct RNA002 legacy chemistry
options.help: Legacy native-RNA workflow for older direct RNA conditions.

options.label: Direct RNA Sequencing Kit (SQK-RNA004)
options.help: Native RNA sequencing without cDNA conversion or PCR.

options.label: cDNA branch / barcoded cDNA
options.help: PCR-cDNA barcoding workflow rather than native direct RNA.

options.label: Standard isolate workflow
options.help: Single-isolate or standard ONT bacterial sequencing.

options.label: Barcoded metagenome
options.help: Multiplexed metagenomic sequencing rather than a single isolate.

options.label: Amplicon workflow
options.help: 12S / 16S or similar amplicon library.

options.label: Signal-only AI workflow
options.help: Raw-signal modeling rather than standard basecalled analysis.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Do not use library mode as a strict differentiator.

questions.label: Are your data multiplexed?
questions.description: Use this to highlight whether a documented demultiplexing branch is likely to matter.

options.label: Yes
options.help: Barcodes are present and samples still need per-sample handling.

options.label: No
options.help: Single-sample or already separated inputs.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Treat multiplexing as neutral.

questions.label: Demultiplexing status
questions.description: This does not choose the pipeline, but it changes how the recommendation is phrased.

options.label: Still needed
options.help: The reads are not yet separated by sample or barcode.

options.label: Already done
options.help: Per-sample files are already available.

options.label: Not needed
options.help: Single-sample data or workflow branch without demultiplexing.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Keep demultiplexing neutral.

questions.label: Basecalling state or preferred Dorado tier
questions.description: Use this to tailor the recommended entry point and ONT Dorado guidance.

options.label: Raw data, not basecalled yet
options.help: You still need a basecalling step.

options.label: Prefer FAST
options.help: Fastest and least compute-intensive; best for quick turnaround when compute is limited.

options.label: Prefer HAC
options.help: ONT's general recommendation for most users as the balance between accuracy and compute cost.

options.label: Prefer SUP
options.help: Highest accuracy and highest compute cost; often useful when assembly or difficult calls matter most.

options.label: Already basecalled
options.help: Reads are already in FASTQ or BAM.

options.label: Not applicable
options.help: Use this for VCF- or signal-only cases where standard basecalling is not the focus.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Keep basecalling state neutral.

questions.label: Adapter trimming and read filtering
questions.description: Use this to tailor whether the workflow should start before or after trimming and filtering.

options.label: Need trimming and filtering
options.help: Adapters and length / quality filtering are still pending.

options.label: Need trimming only
options.help: Adapters still need to be removed, but filtering may already be done.

options.label: Need filtering only
options.help: Reads are trimmed but still need Q / length filtering.

options.label: Already trimmed and filtered
options.help: Use the workflow after preprocessing if thresholds still match.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Keep preprocessing state neutral.
